_sources.discrepancies: Iowa's voter ID law: photo ID is requested but not strictly required — voters without ID may use the poll book affidavit as an alternative. NCSL categorizes Iowa as 'photo ID requested'. Iowa enacted same-day registration; voters may register through Election Day. Iowa 2026 primary date set by statute as the first Tuesday after the first Monday in June.

earlyVoting.notes: Early voting (in-person absentee) begins 29 days before the election at county auditor offices. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting (in-person absentee) begins 29 days before the election. Satellite sites may be available. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting (in-person absentee) begins 29 days before the general election at county auditor offices. Satellite early voting locations may be available. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.

acceptedIds: Iowa driver's license or state ID
Veterans ID card issued by the U.S. Dept. of Veterans Affairs
Iowa voter ID card (free from county auditor)
votingRules.expirationRule: Photo ID must not be expired. Iowa voter ID cards do not expire. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
votingRules.impedimentDeclaration: Voters without an acceptable photo ID may sign an election day affidavit attesting to their identity. A poll worker who knows the voter may also attest to their identity.
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Iowa does not prohibit phones at polling places. Voters may not photograph their ballot in a way that reveals how they voted. No electioneering within 300 feet of the polling place.
additionalRules: Closed primary: only voters registered with a party may vote in that party's primary. Unregistered voters may register on Election Day.
No runoffs in Iowa — the candidate with the most votes wins.
Same-day registration available on Election Day with proof of Iowa residence.

eligibility: Any registered voter may request an absentee ballot without providing a reason
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: 15 days before the election (received by county auditor)
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the county auditor by the time polls close on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive at the county auditor by poll closing on Election Day. Postmarks do not count.

primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEn: Iowa has a semi-closed primary (Iowa Code §43.2). Voters registered with a party vote in their party's primary. Voters registered as no-party (independent) or who change registration on the day of the primary may participate by declaring a party at the polls on election day.
primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEs: Iowa tiene una primaria semi-cerrada (Iowa Code §43.2). Los votantes registrados en un partido votan en la primaria de su partido. Los votantes registrados sin partido (independientes) o que cambian su registro el día de la primaria pueden participar declarando un partido en el lugar de votación el día de las elecciones.
